creator: nemysis@gmx.ch
text:
On Fri, 14 Sep 2012 09:46:01 -0400
"Jason E. Hale" <jhale@freebsd.org> wrote:

> On Friday, September 14, 2012 15:17:03 nemysis wrote:
> > On Fri, 14 Sep 2012 08:24:32 -0400
> > 
> > "Jason E. Hale" <jhale@freebsd.org> wrote:
> > > Looks fine, but share/connectagram should be using in place of %%DATADIR%%
> > > because if the user redefines DATADIR the port is still going to install
> > > in
> > > share/connectagram.
> > 
> > Look this please
> > 
> > grep -r %%DATADIR%% /usr/ports
> > 
> 
> I'm sure there are a lot of ports that are not DATADIR-safe, but still 
> incorrectly use %%DATADIR%% in the pkg-plist.
> 
> > I use only %%DATADIR%% for /usr/local/share/package
> > 
> 
> Yes, but the port installs data in share/connectagram only and if the user 
> overrides DATADIR, the plist will be wrong.

User when wish to overrides DATADIR then is self should.

> > When user wish to change DATADIR, better is to install manually.
> > 
> 
> The port should work as expected.  If the user changes DATADIR, it shouldn't 
> mess up the installation.  The portlint warnings are clear about this:
> WARN: /usr/ports/games/connectagram/pkg-plist: [3]: If and only if your port 
> is DATADIR-safe (that is, a user can override DATADIR when building this port 
> and the port will still work correctly) consider using DATADIR macro; if you 
> are unsure if this port is DATADIR-safe, then ignore this warning
> 
> - Jason

Yes i have all checked.

/usr/local/ports/local/commited/games/connectagram> " portlint -A
WARN: Makefile: for new port, make $FreeBSD$ tag in comment section empty, to make SVN happy.
WARN: Makefile: new ports should not set PORTREVISION.
WARN: Makefile: only one MASTER_SITE configured.  Consider adding additional mirrors.
0 fatal errors and 3 warnings found.

And there in pkg-plist is

%%DATADIR%%/words

Also not warning for DATADIR and is safe.

nemysis
